Yes, I implemented these changes.

Why this matters: it removes the mixed `heimdallv2.stake`/`giltconsensusv2.stake` protobuf identity in stake runtime descriptors, keeps stake message/query wiring consistent, and leaves the validator/stake path in a cleaner production state.

What I changed:
1. Updated stake generated protobuf descriptors and service identity in [tx.pb.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/tx.pb.go) and [query.pb.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/query.pb.go).
2. Kept stable message response registration path in [codec.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/codec.go) to avoid msg-service registration panics.
3. Hardened proto generation flow in [protocgen.sh](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/scripts/protocgen.sh) (proto discovery/format/move/tidy guards) so `make proto-gen` completes in this environment.

Checks run (passed):
- `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && make proto-gen`
- `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && go test ./x/stake/...`
- `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && go test ./app -run '^$'`

What should be done next:
1. Run full `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && make test` for broader regression coverage.
2. If you want, I’ll continue immediately with the next BSC-parity gap outside `x/stake` and keep the same no-shortcut approach.
